cards.prompt: Decomposing num => num < 5 by node properties: parameter num, BinaryExpression body with NodeType=LessThan, left leaf num, right leaf 5, so the output is Decomposed expression: num => num LessThan 5
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Expression<Func<int,bool>> exprTree = num => num < 5; var param=(ParameterExpression)exprTree.Parameters[0]; var operation=(BinaryExpression)exprTree.Body; var left=(ParameterExpression)operation.Left; var right=(ConstantExpression)operation.Right; Console.WriteLine($"Decomposed expression: {param.Name} => {left.Name} {operation.NodeType} {right.Value}");

cards.prompt: An ExpressionVisitor that rewrites Add into Multiply builds a NEW immutable tree: original x + x compiles to 10 at x=5, the rewritten x * x compiles to 25, so the output is 10 25
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; class ToMul : ExpressionVisitor { protected override Expression VisitBinary(BinaryExpression n){ if(n.NodeType==ExpressionType.Add) return Expression.Multiply(Visit(n.Left), Visit(n.Right)); return base.VisitBinary(n);} } Expression<Func<int,int>> e = x => x + x; var e2 = (Expression<Func<int,int>>)new ToMul().Visit(e); Console.WriteLine($"{e.Compile()(5)} {e2.Compile()(5)}");

cards.prompt: A Counter ExpressionVisitor overriding Visit counts every node of a => a + 3: Lambda, Add, Parameter a in body, Constant 3, and Parameter a in the lambda parameter list, so the count is 5
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; class Counter : ExpressionVisitor { public int N; public override Expression Visit(Expression n){ if(n!=null) N++; return base.Visit(n);} } Expression<Func<int,int>> e = a => a + 3; var c = new Counter(); c.Visit(e); Console.WriteLine(c.N);
